prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock price volatility patterns, I used an assumed annualized volatility of approximately 25% for GE. Over 18 days, this translates to a standard deviation of roughly 3.9%. The point estimate assumes no drift (random walk), while confidence intervals are calculated using normal distribution assumptions around the current price, with wider intervals reflecting greater uncertainty over the 18-day horizon.
